title: Did you hear it? Here’s what we know about a big boom heard around Mass.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Residents across Greater Boston reported hearing a loud boom on Saturday afternoon, prompting widespread speculation.
Meteorologists identified the source as a bolide meteor, ruling out earthquakes and thunderstorms as potential causes.
Local safety officials confirmed no emergency incidents related to the boom but received multiple 911 calls across the region.

title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Tyler Samaniego allowed four runs in the first inning during a 4-3 loss to the Guardians.
Brayan Bello excelled in relief, pitching seven scoreless innings with five strikeouts and no walks.
Red Sox offense fell short after closing the gap to 4-3, leaving seven runners on base during the game.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Celtics fans and the atmosphere at TD Garden during a recent livestream.
Boston Celtics and Miami Heat are leading contenders to acquire Antetokounmpo amid ongoing trade rumors.
Speculation includes potential trade involving Jaylen Brown as part of a deal to land Antetokounmpo in Boston.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police cleared Harvard's Senior Sunrise event on Weeks Footbridge due to crowd and safety concerns.
Massachusetts State Police responded to reports of blocked pathways and attempted swimming in the Charles River.
The incident occurred a day after the Class of 2026's commencement, with comedian Conan O'Brien as the speaker.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair' is now streaming on Peacock, featuring Uma Thurman's acclaimed performance.
'The Truman Show' is available on Paramount+ and YouTube, spotlighting Jim Carrey's top-tier acting.
'The Four Seasons' Season 2 and 'Spider-Noir' are streaming on Netflix and Prime Video, respectively.

title: Red Sox predicted to draft 18-year-old third baseman in 2026 MLB Draft by ESPN
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: ESPN's Kiley McDaniel predicts Red Sox will draft Bo Lowrance, an 18-year-old third baseman, in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Bo Lowrance, compared to Freddie Freeman by scouts, is a left-handed hitter committed to University of Virginia.
Lowrance has a 6-foot-5 frame and could switch to first base due to defensive concerns at third.

title: Lowell father and daughter charged in alleged payroll tax scheme
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lim Ou, 69, and Nalen Ou, 39, of Lowell charged with federal tax crimes for alleged payroll tax evasion.
Prosecutors allege the duo concealed over $48 million in cash payroll and failed to pay $12 million in taxes.
They allegedly ran staffing agencies under KHL Inc. and Top Labors Inc., paying workers in cash since 2013.

title: Report: Christian Gonzalez unlikely to participate in OTAs under current Patriots contract
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Christian Gonzalez, Patriots cornerback, is unlikely to participate in OTAs due to pending contract extension negotiations.
Gonzalez, an All-Pro in 2024 and Pro Bowler in 2025, seeks a significant salary increase beyond his current $4 million.
The Patriots restructured Mike Onwenu's contract, freeing cap space for potential extensions or acquisitions.

title: Search underway for driver who led cops on a chase through Everett, Malden, Stoneham
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police are searching for a driver who led a chase through Everett, Malden, and Stoneham, Massachusetts, striking a State Police cruiser.
The suspect, wanted for drug investigation, had multiple outstanding warrants and briefly evaded officers before abandoning the vehicle.
The vehicle was found abandoned in Stoneham; driver and passenger fled on foot, prompting a search with police K-9 units.

title: Ex-Patriots HC Jerod Mayo has a new job away from football
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jerod Mayo, former Patriots head coach, has joined Fifth Down Capital as managing director in February.
Fifth Down Capital is a private equity firm based in Needham, investing in technology, healthcare, and consumer sectors.
Before coaching, Mayo worked in business as Optum's VP of Business Development from 2015 to 2018.
